title: Area & perimeter of rectangles word problems
description: Find the missing side length of a rectangle when given its perimeter or area.  Compare perimeters and areas of rectangles.
topic_path: Area & perimeter of rectangles word problems

assessment_items.question_content:
Margo wants to build a rectangular fence around her garden, and she has $24$ meters of wood.

**Which of these dimensions will give Margo the largest area?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the area of each rectangle to see which one has the largest area.

$\text{Area of a rectangle} = \text{width} \times \text{height}$

###$8$ meters by $4$ meters


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Area}=\greenD8\text{ m}\times\blueD4\text{ m} = 32\text{ square meters}$

###$10$ meters by $2$ meters


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Area}=\greenD{10}\text{ m}\times\blueD2\text{ m} = 20\text{ square meters}$

###$6$ meters by $6$ meters


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Area}=\greenD{6}\text{ m}\times\blueD6\text{ m} = 36\text{ square meters}$
The garden with the dimensions of $6\text{ meters}$ by $6\text{ meters}$ has the largest area.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jordan wants to create a rectangular border around his classroom bulletin board, and he has $30$ meters of border.

**Which of these dimensions will give Jordan the largest area?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the area of each rectangle to see which one has the largest area.

$\text{Area of a rectangle} = \text{width} \times \text{height}$

###$10$ meters by $5$ meters


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Area}=\greenD{10}\text{ m}\times\blueD5\text{ m} = 50\text{ square meters}$

###$7$ meters by $8$ meters


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Area}=\greenD{8}\text{ m}\times\blueD7\text{ m} = 56\text{ square meters}$

###$12$ meters by $3$ meters


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Area}=\greenD{12}\text{ m}\times\blueD3\text{ m} = 36\text{ square meters}$
The bulletin board with the dimensions of $8\text{ meters}$ by $7\text{ meters}$ has the largest area.

assessment_items.question_content:
Chloe has enough sand to fill a rectangular sandbox with an area of $36$ square units. She wants the outer edges of the sandbox to use as little material as possible.

**Which dimensions will give Chloe the smallest perimeter?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find the perimeter of each rectangle to see which one has the smallest perimeter.

$\text{Perimeter} = \text{length} + \text{width} +  \text{length} + \text{width}$

###$6$ units by $6$ units


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter}=\greenD{6}\text{ u}+\blueD6\text{ u} + \greenD{6}\text{ u}+\blueD6\text{ u} = 24\text{ units}$

###$9$ units by $4$ units

[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter}=\greenD{9}\text{ u}+\blueD4\text{ u} + \greenD{9}\text{ u}+\blueD4\text{ u} = 26\text{ units}$

###$12$ units by $3$ units


[[☃ image 1]]

$\text{Perimeter}=\greenD{12}\text{ u}+\blueD3\text{ u} + \greenD{12}\text{ u}+\blueD3\text{ u} = 30\text{ units}$
$24\text{ u} < 26\text{ u}< 30\text{ u}$
A rectanglar sandbox with the dimensions of $6\text{ units}$ by $6\text{ units}$ has the smallest perimeter.

assessment_items.question_content:
The area of a square is $144$ square centimeters.  

**What is the length of one side of the square?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] centimeters
hints:
**Area** is the amount of space a flat shape fills. 

$\text{Area of a square} = \text{width} \times \text{height}$

What number can we multiply by itself to get $\goldE{144}$?

$\goldE{144}=\greenD{s}\times\blueD{s}$
The length of one side of the square is $\greenD{12}$ centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
A square has an area of $100$ square meters.

**What is the perimeter of the square?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] meters
hints:
**Area** is the amount of space a flat shape fills. 

$\text{Area of a square} = \text{width} \times \text{height}$

What number can we multiply by itself to get $\goldE{100}$?

$\goldE{100}=\greenD{s}\times\blueD{s}$

The length of each side is $10$ meters.

Now, we can find the perimeter.  

$\begin{align}
\text{Perimeter} &= \greenD{\text{ side}} + \blueD{\text{ side}} +  \greenD{\text{ side}} + \blueD{\text{ side}}\\\\
&=\greenD{10} + \blueD{10} + \greenD{10} + \blueD{10}\\\\
&=40
\end{align}$
The perimeter of the square is $40$ meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
A square has an area of $49$ square meters.

**What is the perimeter of the square?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] meters
hints:
**Area** is the amount of space a flat shape fills. 

$\text{Area of a square} = \text{width} \times \text{height}$

What number can we multiply by itself to get $\goldE{49}$?

$\goldE{49}=\greenD{s}\times\blueD{s}$

The length of each side is $7$ meters.

Now, we can find the perimeter.  

$\begin{align}
\text{Perimeter} &= \greenD{\text{ side}} + \blueD{\text{ side}} +  \greenD{\text{ side}} + \blueD{\text{ side}}\\\\
&=\greenD7 + \blueD7 + \greenD7 + \blueD7\\\\
&=28
\end{align}$
The perimeter of the square is $28$ meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Steve built a square hamster pen that has a perimeter of $240$ centimeters.

**What is the length of one side of Steve's hamster pen?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] centimeters
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around a closed figure. 

We can find the perimeter of a shape by adding all the side lengths.

The perimeter of the square is $\purpleD{240}\text{ cm}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\purpleD{P} &= \greenD{s} + \blueD{s} + \greenD{s} + \blueD{s}\\\\
\purpleD{240} &=  \greenD{s}  + \greenD{s} + \blueD{s} + \blueD{s}\\\\
\purpleD{240} &= 4 \times s
\end{align}$
What number can we multiply by $4$ to get $\purpleD{240}$?

We can divide to find the side length.  

$\purpleD{240}\div4= \blueD{\text{s}}$
One side of the hamster pen is $\blueD{60}$ centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The perimeter of Jonah's square backyard is $56$ meters.

**What is the area of Jonah's backyard?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] square meters
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around a closed figure. 

We can find the perimeter of a shape by adding all the side lengths.

The perimeter of the square is $\purpleD{56}\text{ m}$.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\begin{align}
\purpleD{P} &= \greenD{s} + \blueD{s} + \greenD{s} + \blueD{s}\\\\
\purpleD{56} &=  \greenD{s}  + \greenD{s} + \blueD{s} + \blueD{s}\\\\
\purpleD{56} &= 4 \times s
\end{align}$
What number can we multiply by $4$ to get $\purpleD{56}$?

Now, we can find the area. 

$\begin{align}
\text{Area} &= \text{side} \times \text{side}\\\\
&= \blueD{s} \times \blueD{s}\\\\
&= \blueD{14} \times \blueD{14}\\\\
&= 196\text{ square meters}
\end{align}$
The area of Jonah's backyard is $196$ square meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
A square has a perimeter of $36$ millimeters.

**What is the area of the square?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] square millimeters
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around a closed figure. 

We can find the perimeter of a shape by adding all the side lengths.

The perimeter of the square is $\purpleD{36}\text{ mm}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\purpleD{P} &= \greenD{s} + \blueD{s} + \greenD{s} + \blueD{s}\\\\
\purpleD{36} &=  \greenD{s}  + \greenD{s} + \blueD{s} + \blueD{s}\\\\
\purpleD{36} &= 4 \times s
\end{align}$
What number can we multiply by $4$ to get $\purpleD{36}$?

Now, we can find the area. 

$\begin{align}
\text{Area} &= \text{side} \times \text{side}\\\\
&= \blueD{s} \times \blueD{s}\\\\
&= \blueD{9} \times \blueD{9}\\\\
&= 81\text{ square mm}
\end{align}$
The area of the square is $81$ square millimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The area of a rectangle is $117$ square meters.  The width is $9$ meters.

**What is the length of the rectangle?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] meters
hints:
**Area** is the amount of space a flat shape fills.

$\text{Area of a rectangle} = \text{width} \times \text{length}$

The **$\goldE{\text{area}}$** of the rectangle is $\goldE{117}$ square meters and the $\blueD{\text{width}}$ is $\blueD{9}$ meters.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\goldE{\text{area}} &= \greenD{\ell}\times\blueD{w}\\\\
\goldE{117} &= \greenD{\ell}\times\blueD{9}\\\\
\goldE{117}\div\blueD9&=\greenD{\ell}
\end{align}$
The length of the rectangle is $\greenD{13}$ meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jason built a rectangular tool shed that is $8$ meters wide and has an area of $96$ square meters.  

**What is the length of Jason’s tool shed?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] meters
hints:
**Area** is the amount of space a flat shape fills.

$\text{Area of a rectangle} = \text{width} \times \text{length}$

The **$\goldE{\text{area}}$** of the rectangle is $\goldE{96}$ square meters and the $\blueD{\text{width}}$ is $\blueD{8}$ meters.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\goldE{\text{area}} &= \greenD{\ell}\times\blueD{w}\\\\
\goldE{96} &= \greenD{\ell}\times\blueD{8}\\\\
\goldE{96}\div\blueD8&=\greenD{\ell}
\end{align}$
The length of the tool shed is $\greenD{12}$ meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The area of a rectangle is $28$ square meters.  The length is $7$ meters.

**What is the width of the rectangle?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] meters
hints:
**Area** is the amount of space a flat shape fills.

$\text{Area of a rectangle} = \text{width} \times \text{height}$

The **$\goldE{\text{area}}$** of the rectangle is $\goldE{28}$ square meters and the $\greenD{\text{length}}$ is $\greenD{7}$ meters.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\goldE{\text{area}} &= \greenD{7}\times\blueD{w}\\\\
\goldE{28} &= \greenD{7}\times\blueD{w}\\\\
\goldE{28}\div\greenD7&=\blueD{w}
\end{align}$
The width of the rectangle is $\blueD{4}$ meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Elise built a large rectangular vegetable garden that is $9$ meters long and has an area of $72$ square meters.  

**What is the perimeter of Elise’s vegetable garden?**

[[☃ input-number 2]] meters
hints:
**Area** is the amount of space a flat shape fills.

$\text{Area of a rectangle} = \text{width} \times \text{length}$

The **$\goldE{\text{area}}$** of the rectangle is $\goldE{72}$ square meters and the $\greenD{\text{length}}$ is $\greenD{9}$ meters.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\goldE{\text{area}} &= \greenD{9}\times\blueD{w}\\\\
\goldE{72} &= \greenD{9}\times\blueD{w}\\\\
\goldE{72}\div\greenD9&=\blueD{w}\\\\
\blueD8&=\blueD{w}
\end{align}$

Now, we can find the perimeter.  

$\begin{align}
\text{Perimeter} &= \greenD{\text{ length}} + \blueD{\text{ width}} +  \greenD{\text{ length}} + \blueD{\text{ width}}\\\\
&=\greenD9 + \blueD8 + \greenD9 + \blueD8\\\\
&=34
\end{align}$
The perimeter of the garden is $34$ meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The area of a rectangle is $42$ square millimeters.  The length is $7$ millimeters.

**What is the perimeter of the rectangle?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] millimeters
hints:
**Area** is the amount of space a flat shape fills.

$\text{Area of a rectangle} = \text{width} \times \text{length}$

The **$\goldE{\text{area}}$** of the rectangle is $\goldE{42}$ square millimeters and the $\greenD{\text{length}}$ is $\greenD{7}$ millimeters.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\goldE{\text{area}} &= \greenD{7}\times\blueD{w}\\\\
\goldE{42} &= \greenD{7}\times\blueD{w}\\\\
\goldE{42}\div\greenD7&=\blueD{w}\\\\
\blueD6&=\blueD{w}
\end{align}$

Now, we can find the perimeter.  

$\begin{align}
\text{Perimeter} &= \greenD{\text{ length}} + \blueD{\text{ width}} +  \greenD{\text{ length}} + \blueD{\text{ width}}\\\\
&=\greenD7 + \blueD6 + \greenD7 + \blueD6\\\\
&=26
\end{align}$
The perimeter of the rectangle is $26$ millimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Judy built a rectangular patio that is $9$ meters wide and has a perimeter of $40$ meters.  

**What is the length of Judy's patio?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] meters
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around a closed figure. 

We can find the perimeter of a shape by adding all the side lengths.

The perimeter is $\purpleD{40}\text{ m}$ and the width is $\blueD{9}\text{ m}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\purpleD{P} &= \greenD{l} + \blueD{w} + \greenD{l} + \blueD{w}\\\\
\purpleD{40} &= \greenD{\ell} + \blueD9 +  \greenD{\ell} + \blueD9 \\\\
\purpleD{40} &=  \greenD{\ell}  + \greenD{\ell} + \blueD{18}\\\\
22 &= \greenD{\ell}  + \greenD{\ell}
\end{align}$
What number can we add twice to get $22$?
The length of the patio is $\greenD{11}$ meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The perimeter of a rectangle is $90$ centimeters.  The length is $27$ centimeters.

**What is the width of the rectangle?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] centimeters
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around a closed figure. 

We can find the perimeter of a shape by adding all the side lengths.

The perimeter is $\purpleD{90}\text{ cm}$ and the length is $\greenD{27}\text{ cm}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\purpleD{P} &= \greenD{l} + \blueD{w} + \greenD{l} + \blueD{w}\\\\
\purpleD{90} &= \greenD{27} + \blueD{w} +  \greenD{27} + \blueD{w} \\\\
\purpleD{90} &=  \greenD{54} + \blueD{w} + \blueD{w}\\\\
36 &=  \blueD{w} + \blueD{w}
\end{align}$
What number can we add twice to get $36$?
The width of the rectangle is $\blueD{18}$ centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lisa built a rectangular flower garden that is $4$ meters wide and has a perimeter of $26$ meters.  

**What is the length of Lisa's flower garden?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] meters
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around a closed figure. 

We can find the perimeter of a shape by adding all the side lengths.

The perimeter is $\purpleD{26}\text{ m}$ and the width is $\blueD{4}\text{ m}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\purpleD{P} &= \greenD{l} + \blueD{w} + \greenD{l} + \blueD{w}\\\\
\purpleD{26} &= \greenD{\ell} + \blueD4 +  \greenD{\ell} + \blueD4 \\\\
\purpleD{26} &=  \greenD{\ell}  + \greenD{\ell} + \blueD{8}\\\\
18&= \greenD{\ell}  + \greenD{\ell}
\end{align}$
What number can we add twice to get $18$?
The length of the flower garden is $\greenD{9}$ meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The perimeter of a rectangle is $20$ centimeters.  The length is $6$ centimeters.

**What is the area of the rectangle?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] square centimeters
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around a closed figure. 

We can find the perimeter of a shape by adding all the side lengths.

The perimeter is $\purpleD{20}\text{ cm}$ and the length is $\greenD{6}\text{ cm}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\purpleD{P} &= \greenD{l} + \blueD{w} + \greenD{l} + \blueD{w}\\\\
\purpleD{20} &= \greenD{6} + \blueD{w} +  \greenD{6} + \blueD{w} \\\\
\purpleD{20} &=  \blueD{w}  + \blueD{w} + \greenD{12}\\\\
8&= \blueD{w} + \blueD{w}
\end{align}$

What number can we add twice to get $8$?

The width of the rectangle is $\blueD{4}$ centimeters.
The area of the rectangle is $24$ square centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The perimeter of a rectangle is $32$ centimeters.  The width is $7$ centimeters.

**What is the area of the rectangle?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] square centimeters
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around a closed figure. 

We can find the perimeter of a shape by adding all the side lengths.

The perimeter is $\purpleD{32}\text{ cm}$ and the width is $\blueD{7}\text{ cm}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\purpleD{P} &= \greenD{l} + \blueD{w} + \greenD{l} + \blueD{w}\\\\
\purpleD{32} &= \greenD{\ell} + \blueD7 +  \greenD{\ell} + \blueD7 \\\\
\purpleD{32} &=  \greenD{\ell}  + \greenD{\ell} + \blueD{14}\\\\
18&= \greenD{\ell}  + \greenD{\ell}
\end{align}$

What number can we add twice to get $18$?

The length of the rectangle is $\greenD{9}$ centimeters.
The area of the rectangle is $63$ square centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
A rectangle has a width of $3$ units.  The rectangle's length is $5$ times its width.  

**What is the area of the rectangle?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] square units
hints:
**Area** is the amount of space a flat shape fills. 

$\text{Area of a rectangle} = \text{width} \times \text{length}$
The $\blueD{\text{ width}}$ is $\blueD{3}\text{ units}$ and the $\greenD{\text{length}}$ is $5$ times the $\blueD{\text{width}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The $\greenD{\text{length}}$ is $5\times\blueD3=\greenD{15}$ units.


[[☃ image 1]]

Now, we can find the area.

$\begin{align}
\goldE{\text{Area}} &= \greenD{l} \times \blueD{w}\\\\ 
&= \greenD{15}\times \blueD{3}\\\\
&=45
\end{align}$
The area of the rectangle is $45$ square units.

assessment_items.question_content:
A rectangular playground is $8$ meters wide.  It is $3$ times as long as it is wide.

**What is the area of the playground?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] square meters
hints:
**Area** is the amount of space a flat shape fills. 

$\text{Area of a rectangle} = \text{width} \times \text{length}$

The $\blueD{\text{width}}$ is $\blueD{8}\text{ meters}$ and the $\greenD{\text{length}}$ is $3$ times the $\blueD{\text{width}}$.




[[☃ image 1]]

The $\greenD{\text{length}}$ is $3\times\blueD{8}=\greenD{24}\text{ meters}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

Now, we can find the area.

$\begin{align}
\goldE{\text{Area}} &= \greenD{l} \times \blueD{w}\\\\ 
&= \greenD{24}\times \blueD{8}\\\\
&=192
\end{align}$
The area of the playground is $192$ square meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
A rectangle has a width of $10$ centimeters.  The rectangle's length is $4$ times its width.  

**What is the perimeter of the rectangle?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] centimeters
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around a closed figure. 

We can find the perimeter of a shape by adding all the side lengths.

The $\blueD{\text{width}}$ is $\blueD{10}\text{ cm}$ and the $\greenD{\text{length}}$ is $4$ times the $\blueD{\text{width}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The $\greenD{\text{length}}$ is $4\times\blueD{10}=\greenD{40}\text{ cm}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

Now, we can find the perimeter.  

$\begin{align}
\text{Perimeter} &= \greenD{\text{ length}} + \blueD{\text{ width}} +  \greenD{\text{ length}} + \blueD{\text{ width}}\\\\
&=\greenD{40}+ \blueD{10} + \greenD{40} + \blueD{10}\\\\
&=100
\end{align}$
The perimeter of the rectangle is $100$ centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
A rectangular sandbox has a width of $5$ units.  The sandbox is $5$ times as long as it is wide.  

**What is the perimeter of the sandbox?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] units
hints:
**Perimeter** is the distance around a closed figure. 

We can find the perimeter of a shape by adding all the side lengths.

The $\blueD{\text{width}}$ is $\blueD{5}\text{ units}$ and the $\greenD{\text{length}}$ is $5$ times the $\blueD{\text{width}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The $\greenD{\text{length}}$ is $5\times\blueD{5}=\greenD{25}$ units.


[[☃ image 1]]

Now, we can find the perimeter.  

$\begin{align}
\text{Perimeter} &= \greenD{\text{ length}} + \blueD{\text{ width}} +  \greenD{\text{ length}} + \blueD{\text{ width}}\\\\
&=\greenD{25}+ \blueD{5} + \greenD{25} + \blueD{5}\\\\
&=60
\end{align}$
The perimeter of the sandbox is $60$ units.
